publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-10-02 18:03:37 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Correspondence, notebooks, exams, manuscripts, clippings, scrapbooks, diaries, certificates, diplomas, family genealogy.
did.abstract: Correspondence pertaining to von Engeln's years at Cornell as a student and professor and to his writings on geology and Cornell history.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

arrangement.head: SERIES LIST
arrangement.p: Series II. Ralph S. Tarr Memorial Box 1
Series III. Cornell and miscellaneous Boxes 1, 8, 10, 18
Series VII. Certificates and diplomas Box 8, Mapcase Folder

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Oscar Diedrich von Engeln papers, #14-15-856. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and content
scopecontent.p: Correspondence pertaining to von Engeln's years at Cornell as a student and professor and to his writings on geology and Cornell history; student notebooks and exams; manuscripts of published and unpublished works; ice experiment notes by John L. Rich, 1911, later published by von Engeln, 1914; ice surface rubbings; "Records of Physiographical Experiments," 1915-1917; photographs and lantern slides of family, friends, colleagues and students, geological subjects, and world travels, including oversized glass plates and negatives of New York State Relief Maps; von Engeln's camera; photographs of natural features in Turkestan taken by W. Rickmer Rickmers, with related correspondence; clippings; scrapbooks, diaries; certificates and diplomas; and a family genealogy. Material pertaining to Ralph S. Tarr, Professor of Geology, and reminiscences of other Geology Department members. Also, letters (including one from T. C. Chamberlin), unpublished articles, notes, two diaries, and photograph album (with identification) concerning Alaskan trips in 1906 and 1907.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and notes on Cayuga Rock Salt Company
Letter from T. C. Chamberlin to Oscar von Engeln
Correspondence regarding glacial lake history of the Cayuga Valley
Series II. Ralph S. Tarr Memorial
Items pertaining to the death of Ralph S. Tarr
Ralph S. Tarr Memorial: lists, letters, financial records
Ralph S. Tarr Memorial: suggestions re inscriptions
Expenses of the Deaprtment of Physical Geography and Summer School Instruction
"Cornell University Summer Session Geography and Geology" (draft)
History pf Cornell University Department of Geology
Book of Letters to von Engeln
Diary, Mrs. von Engeln (formerly Maude Graham Hewitt)
Gold stick pin, belonged to Prof. O.D. Von Engeln, oval outline with 2 narrow bars across the center, Sigma Xi
Oval shaped pin with an open book and the words "The Cornell Lion", gold inscription on back: "D. Von Engeln 1908", Sigma Xi
Gold Sigma Xi key with "O.D. Von Engeln" on back
Camera used by O. D. Von Engeln in Alaska
Lewis B. Kelsey: term paper in Special Problems Geol. 208
Principles of Geology, mss. foreword through chapter 3
Interglacial deposit in Central New York, manuscript handed to the Secretary of the Geological Society
Autochrome of von Engeln and "Buzzie" with Helen Ogden

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also includes photographs of travel to the Grand Canyon, Mexico, Venezuela, Barbados, Martinique, and Italy.

did.unittitle: Alaska expedition and Cornell Enfield excursion
Kalta-Karamk (just before Kizil-sn enters western gorge)
Kalta-Karamk (eastern end of this broad section of Kisil-sn)
View from Farna-Kub above Pashimgan to Darvaz
Peter the Great and Zerin-Zamin from Range of Little Tupchek
Malloryville esker and Malloryville kames glass slides
Topographic sections of New York State on o/s glass negatives, two photocopies of prints of maps [unidentified]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Include Senior Singing, Agricultural College, freshman year posters, Cornell invitations.

did.unittitle: Richford from Morainic Knoll looking west diagonal slide up Truncated Slope
Truncated Spur and slightly modified Valley on east side of Horesheads Elmira, trough at Horseheads
Cortland, NY - South of glacially scoured divide notch - see maps, also note - Virgical continuation of this valley
Labrador Hollow - West end from summit of hill west of village
Rock City, Olean-blocks of Olean conglomerate tilting down hill due to weathering Alleghany Plateau level to south and west
Rock City, Olean - look NE surface of conglomerate and joints
Near Headwaters of Chenango Creek, NY - view from road ro right leading up to 1761' elevation. This is on Taylor Valley region, south of Fulton
Danby - looking down State Road toward East Notch and toward north wall of Danby Trough
Six Mile Creek - Entrance bridge in park
Dunde Region - Pony Hollow and Jackson Hollow Saddle - SW of Newfield

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes images of Keuka, Conesus, Honeoye and Canadice lakes. See Box 5 for prints of some negatives.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to film negatives restricted to the permission of the curator.

did.unittitle: Moraine look at Reynoldsville-Bennettsburg - one from Cayuta Valley from hill on Nicklesburg Road film negative
Divide Area - obsequent and consequent streamheads near Candor - on Southside of Candor - Van Ecttem Valley - First road west of Candor film negative
Negatives of Oct. trip with Wold
Jonet Face Univ. Quarty - Jonet Face of recok bing quarried for Taylor Hall University Quarry - bottom of east side of road
Truncated Spur - west side of Six Mile Valley from Coddington Road
Tributary Valley north of Bold Hill on east side of Six Mile Valley
Up Valley at Sweden Valley, PA - looks NE to NE - no ice glacial
Excursion to Divide area around Cayuta, also Moraine Channel near Newfield, Oct.
Whitstone Gulf Butresses - This gorge is so large because eroded just glacially in very weak shales
Overflow southward into Unadilla Creek Headwaters of high level lake founded in slope of esearpment
Terrace along east side of outlet - highway crossing near junction with Pony Hollow
Downstream toward Cayuta Outlet (south) Terrance on west side of lower end (from highway toward Pony Hollow)
Doubtful bedrock near divide, upheaved or transported? (lower gorge, near divide)
Place of divide, near position of photo above, looking downstream (lower gorge, divide)
Swamp at divide point looking south (lower gorge)
Ledge rock fall in tributary valley, NE side of gorge (above divides, lower gorge)
View of north end of lower gorge toward SE
View of lower gorge valley from north end southward, hill in distance, flat area, winding, local waste
Cayuta Gorge (middle) outlet - junction with lower gorge. (middle gorge outwashed at mouth, junction)
Upper Cayuta Gorge outlet north of junction with middle gorge (south of road bridge)
Fall hill east of which flows out outlet channel, head of middle gorge, west of which lies present outlet channel (along road about one mile north of Alpine)
Ithaca area and Cornell film negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes travel postcards from Hawaii, Death Valley, Japan, China, Philippines, Singapore, Penang, Malaya, Egypt, France, Monaco, Italy, and Germany.

did.unittitle: Corporal, New York, State 4th Infantry, Company D

Acknowledgement of Mrs. von Engeln's service with the Fifth Liberty Loan
von Engeln elected a member of the American Association for the Advancement of Science
American Association for the Advancement of Science Fellow
Recognition of Mrs. von Engeln's services with the Red Cross
Maude Graham Hewitt, academic diploma from Margaretville, N.Y., High School
Maude Graham Hewitt, high school diploma, Margaretville, N.Y., High School
Maude Graham Hewitt, advanced academic diploma, Margaretville, N.Y., High School
Oscar von Engeln, B.A. from Cornell University
Maude Graham Hewitt, B.A. from Cornell University
Marriage certificate of von Engeln and Maude Graham Hewitt
Oscar von Engeln, Ph.D. from Cornell Unievrsity
Maude Graham Hewitt, initiation certificate from Delta Zeta Sorority
Paper designating von Engeln United States delegate to the international Geographical Congress, held in Warsaw beginning Aug. 23, 1934
Unpublished articles, notes, two diaries, and a photo album concerning Alaskan expeditions
